impl_iters.summary: Implemented SessionInputGateway and routed restore/WebSocket input through it while preserving SessionManager.writeInput as the low-level PTY writer.

Resolved prickly review findings: sanitized caller metadata before writeInput, supplied real WebSocket replay/screen-repair state to the gateway, added screen-repair barrier integration coverage, and required submit scope for submit intent even without newline bytes.

findings.issue: WebSocket and restore gateway adapters used fabricated live bindings and constant replay state.
Caller-provided metadata bypassed gateway sanitization before writeInput.
Gateway tests did not cover WsRouter/restore integration risk.
Submit intent without newline was accepted without mcp:message.submit scope.

dod_check.item: All declared ingress paths route through SessionInputGateway before low-level PTY write.
Replay and screen-repair barriers reject before PTY write and preserve existing WebSocket reject contract.
MCP/open-agent submit intent requires mcp:message.submit scope, including submit requests without literal newline bytes.
Gateway audit/write metadata remains sanitized and raw token, webhook key, full URL, and prompt material do not leak.
User typing/local echo/prompt redraw still keeps AI TUI sessions idle unless substantive command output occurs.

pending_mutations.reason: Worker write scope excludes direct docs/spec mutation side effects; automated evidence recorded in task-owned artifacts instead.
